segments.text: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin that grace may abound?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Certainly not! How shall we who died to sin live any longer in it?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Or do you not know that as many of us as were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore we were buried with Him through baptism into death, that just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For if we have been united together in the likeness of His death, certainly we also shall be in the likeness of His resurrection,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: knowing this, that our old man was crucified with Him, that the body of sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves of sin.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For he who has died has been freed from sin.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with Him,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: knowing that Christ, having been raised from the dead, dies no more. Death no longer has dominion over Him.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the death that He died, He died to sin once for all; but the life that He lives, He lives to God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Likewise you also, reckon yourselves to be dead indeed to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body, that you should obey it in its lusts.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And do not present your members as instruments of unrighteousness to sin, but present yourselves to God as being alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness to God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For sin shall not have dominion over you, for you are not under law but under grace.
segments.speaker: Paul

verses.title: From Slaves of Sin to Slaves of God

segments.text: What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? Certainly not!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Do you not know that to whom you present yourselves slaves to obey, you are that one's slaves whom you obey, whether of sin leading to death, or of obedience leading to righteousness?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But God be thanked that though you were slaves of sin, yet you obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine to which you were delivered.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And having been set free from sin, you became slaves of righteousness.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you presented your members as slaves of uncleanness, and of lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves of righteousness for holiness.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: What fruit did you have then in the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But now having been set free from sin, and having become slaves of God, you have your fruit to holiness, and the end, everlasting life.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
segments.speaker: Paul
